description: Fig. 9 presents combined SEM morphology and EDS elemental mapping of Fe–30Mn–10Al–1.5C–1.5Be low-density steel after hot rolling at 1050 °C. Subfigure (a) shows the SEM image revealing the junction between austenite and ferrite phases, with a rectangular region A highlighting an elongated strip-shaped structure. Subfigures (b–e) display the corresponding EDS elemental distribution maps for Fe (magenta), Mn (cyan), C (dark), and Al (yellow-brown), respectively. The EDS maps demonstrate that the inner strip-shaped structure (region A) is enriched in both Mn and Al, while the outer region of the strip shows Mn enrichment but Al depletion. According to the caption and text, this elongated Mn-rich and Al-rich structure corresponds to the ferrite (δ-ferrite) phase in the 1.5Be steel, surrounded by austenitic matrix. The carbon map shows relatively weak signal intensity. All images share a 1 μm scale bar, indicating microstructural features at the micrometer scale. This figure provides critical evidence for identifying the dual-phase microstructure and elemental partitioning between ferrite and austenite in Be-bearing low-density steels.
